text.title: LinkedIn Founder Post Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of LinkedIn Founder Post Template MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Each post MUST use exactly one of seven hook types: contrarian, postmortem, customer-quote, day-in-life, mistake, mini-framework, prediction. Multi-hook posts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-hook posts are 3x more readable; multi-hook posts read as listicles and lose the founder voice.

rule.statement: Post body MUST be 180-220 words. Below 180 reads as tweet, above 220 reads as article.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn algorithm rewards dwell-time in this range; outside it dwell-time collapses.

rule.statement: No URL in the post body. CTA URL lives in first comment only.
rule.rationale: LinkedIn algorithm down-ranks posts with body links by 30-50%; comment placement preserves reach.

rule.statement: Post MUST have exactly one CTA (book / subscribe / DM / comment-keyword). Multi-CTA posts dilute action.
rule.rationale: Multi-CTA posts halve click-through; conversion analytics confirm single-CTA wins.

rule.statement: Post body MUST cite >=1 piece of lived evidence (specific number, named customer, dated event). Generic prose is rejected.
rule.rationale: Specifics distinguish founder voice from AI-generated content; readers tune out generic prose within 8 seconds.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
